name: Mixed Technical Jargon — Proteomics Workflow
input: Create a workflow diagram for a quantitative proteomics experiment using TMT labeling. Steps: Sample preparation (cell lysis in RIPA buffer, BCA protein assay, reduction with DTT, alkylation with IAA), Enzymatic digestion (trypsin/Lys-C, 37°C overnight, C18 cleanup), TMT labeling (TMT-10plex, label check by LC-MS/MS), Fractionation (high-pH reversed-phase chromatography, 12 fractions), LC-MS/MS analysis (nanoLC-MS/MS on Orbitrap Exploris 480, 120min gradient, DDA mode top20), Data analysis (MaxQuant/Proteome Discoverer, FDR < 1%, log2 fold change > 1.5, adjusted p-value < 0.05), Validation (Western blot for top 5 candidates, PRM targeted assay). Include quality control checkpoints after each major step.
qualityCriteria: All 7 major steps present (Sample prep, Digestion, TMT labeling, Fractionation, LC-MS/MS, Data analysis, Validation)
Technical abbreviations preserved (TMT, LC-MS/MS, DTT, IAA, BCA, DDA, FDR, PRM)
Quality control checkpoints shown between major steps
Sequential workflow is clear (top-down or left-right)
No truncated technical terms (Orbitrap Exploris 480 fully spelled)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: Linear workflow with 7 major blocks, QC diamonds between steps, technical detail preserved in node labels, professional proteomics workflow appearance.
